comment.body:
I tell you this again:
trading is not a guessing-competition.
it is not a "who is right" game. 
Market is not always predictable.
You get the levels, possibilities and probabilities, and play that.

This behavior of trying to time and predict everything is a bad furu-behavior.
Stop it please.
I explained yesterday why hit-rate doesn't matter.

comment.body:
This tells me that the market doesn’t expect disinflationary print. It expects either a neutral/in-line print or a moderately inflationary print, but not a disinflationary one. 
A neutral print would further expand the risk window into Jackson Hole, because market needs PPI too to prove the trend that CPI hints. This is key.
comment.raw_body:
This tells me that the market doesn’t expect disinflationary print. It expects either a neutral/in-line print or a moderately inflationary print, but not a disinflationary one.
